strengths: Three specific, realâworld objects are clearly identified with contextual details, demonstrating strong grounding of the exhibition.
Each object is explicitly linked to the chosen prompt (âHow can we distinguish between knowledge, belief and opinion?â), fulfilling the requirement to explain connections.
Justifications for inclusion are supported by evidence such as publication details, historical provenance and personal context, showing competent use of TOK conventions.
Effective visual choices (textbook photograph, icon image, article screenshot) enhance clarity and anchor the discussion in realâworld artefacts.
Attention to citation style in the Works Cited demonstrates awareness of academic conventions.
weaknesses: Explanations of links to the prompt are largely surfaceâlevel; critical depth on the provisional nature of knowledge and biases is lacking.
Arguments at times drift into description rather than focused analysis (e.g., leadership theories, textbook categorisation).
Inâtext referencing is sparse and inconsistent, reducing the strength of evidence attribution.
Some sections are truncated or repetitive (especially definitions), affecting coherence and clarity.
The conclusion does not synthesise insights across all three objects, limiting the overall coherence of the exhibition.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptor for band 5â6 by clearly identifying three specific objects with realâworld contexts, describing links to the IA prompt, and offering justifications supported by evidence. However, the analysis remains uneven in depth and precision: critical exploration of knowledge versus belief or opinion is only superficially addressed, inâtext citations are inconsistent, and some explanatory sections drift into description or repetition. These limitations prevent a higher band but justify a solid topâofâband score of 6, reflecting competent fulfilment of the criteria with room for deeper TOK analysis and tighter structure.

annotations.comment: Object 1 is a specific, tangible textbook directly linked to your personal context, which strengthens the real-world grounding of your exhibition.

The description of the textbookâs usefulness is descriptive rather than analytical. You could engage with curriculum bias or the provisional nature of textbook knowledge to create a more critical link to the prompt.
The extended discussion on qualitative theories shows an attempt at nuance, but the argument drifts into general description. Consider tightening the focus to directly address your prompt.
The studentâs choice of prompt is clearly stated, establishing focus for the exhibition. This demonstrates a strong grasp of the taskâs requirements and effectively frames subsequent discussions.

The initial definitions of knowledge, belief, and opinion are overly simplistic. Consider incorporating TOK concepts such as the provisional nature of knowledge and the role of justification to deepen your foundational distinctions.
The photograph of the textbook is an effective visual that anchors the real-world context, enhancing clarity about object provenance.

Labelling the textbook as a âknowledge statementâ is asserted but not unpacked. Explore how justification, evidence, and potential biases affect whether textbook content constitutes stable knowledge.
Your recognition that leadership theories blend belief and evidence is insightful. This demonstrates growing analytical depth in distinguishing knowledge from belief.
Object 2 is well-situated with clear museum context. Yet you need to explicitly tie the iconâs historical evidence and faith elements back to the prompt rather than implying the connection.
The biographical details of Simon Ushakov and the iconâs provenance add specificity, but you stop short of analysing source reliability or the iconâs interpretative weight.
The continuation of Object 2 description appears truncated mid-thought. Ensure complete, well-structured sentences to maintain clarity and coherence.
The general TOK definitions on page 3 lack direct application to your icon. Apply these concepts specifically to the icon rather than presenting abstract definitions.
The paragraph on logic and empirical data outlines key distinctions, but it would be stronger if you illustrated these points using elements of the iconâs history or design.
Object 3âs image is relevant and effectively chosen, representing the media article clearly. This adds variety to your exhibitionâs real-world contexts.
The bibliographic details of the Guardian article are clear, but more critical reflection is needed on its status as opinion versus interpretative knowledge.
The continuation of the article description overlaps with your earlier definitions. To avoid repetition, focus each paragraph on one key analytical insight relevant to the prompt.
Your personal context for reading the article adds authenticity. However, you need to explicitly discuss how that context shapes the distinction between opinion and knowledge.
The discussion of Bridleâs position identifies his opinion but stops short of critically evaluating the strength of his evidence. Analyse specific claims to support deeper critique.
Your statement about the articleâs dual nature (facts vs opinion) is apt. This draws a clear contrast relevant to the prompt.
The concluding remarks do not explicitly tie each object back to the central question. Consider adding a brief summary that synthesizes your insights across all three objects.
The Works Cited section demonstrates attention to academic conventions. Ensure consistency of citation style and include in-text referencing to strengthen source attribution.
The museumâs online source is accurately cited with access date. This enhances the credibility of your historical context.
While the iconâs source is cited, integrate this reference within the body to show how you evaluate the museumâs credibility in supporting your analysis.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
